⚖️ Terms of Use & Legal
▼
KRANTZ OUTDOORS KRUGER FIELD GUIDE — TERMS OF USE
1. Data Ownership
All content in this guide — including species data, field intelligence, GPS co-ordinates, camp and facility information, photography intel, route descriptions, and sighting data submitted through this app — is the intellectual property of Krantz Outdoors. Sightings data submitted by users may be used anonymously to improve the guide and product. User personal data is never sold.
2. No Reproduction or Scraping
This guide may not be copied, scraped, reverse-engineered, republished or used to train AI models without written permission from Krantz Outdoors. The format, structure, field intelligence framework and brand identity are proprietary. Licensing enquiries: basecamp@krantzoutdoors.com.
3. GPS & Location Data
This app uses your device location only for the map and travel calculator features. Location data is not stored on any server and is never shared with third parties. You may disable location access at any time in your device settings without affecting core app functionality.
4. Wildlife Safety Disclaimer
This guide is for informational purposes only. All wildlife encounters carry inherent risk. Krantz Outdoors accepts no liability for injury, loss or damage resulting from the use of information in this guide. Always follow SANParks regulations. Never leave your vehicle outside designated areas. Keep a safe distance from all animals.
5. SANParks Rules Override
SANParks regulations, gate times, speed limits and conservation rules supersede all information in this guide. When in doubt, always defer to the official SANParks notice boards, reception staff and rangers. This guide is a planning and reference tool — not an official SANParks publication.
6. Accuracy
Every effort is made to keep information current, but facilities, roads, gate times and species status change. Verify critical information (fuel, medical, gate hours) directly with SANParks before travel. Krantz Outdoors is not liable for decisions made on the basis of outdated information.
© 2026 Krantz Outdoors. All rights reserved. Wild by Nature. Africa by Choice.

v5.16 — BUILD NOTES
• FIXED: Daily Challenge — tap any animal card to open species & log sighting without leaving Games
• FIXED: Daily Challenge — ticked immediately after logging, no need to navigate away and back
• FIXED: My Trail popups — star ratings removed
• FIXED: Leaflet JS preloads on app start — Nav no longer hangs on slow signal
• FIXED: OSRM route fetch 8s timeout — falls back to straight-line on poor signal

Camera Settings Cheat Sheet
🌙
Golden Hour Settings
05:30-08:00 and 16:30-18:30
Prime time
Mode
Aperture Priority
Aperture
f/5.6 - f/7.1 for big cats. f/8 for landscape.
ISO
400-1600 dawn. 200-400 late morning.
Shutter
1/800s minimum for moving subjects. 1/1600s for action.
White balance
Shade (7500K) or Auto. Shade warms the amber light correctly.
Pro tip
Expose for the highlight on the animal's face. Recover shadows in post. Never blow the catchlight in the eye.
☀
Heat Shimmer Management
After 09:30 on open plains
Important
Problem
Heat shimmer distorts telephoto images above 400mm after 09:30 on open plains
Window
05:30-09:30 for clean telephoto. After 09:30 move to woodland and shade.
Workaround
Shoot from vehicle windows, not roof. Lower angle = less heat shimmer path.
Focal test
200mm clean until 10:30. 400mm until 09:30. 500mm+ until 09:00 on hot days. Plan drives accordingly.
🔍
Vehicle as Hide - Technique
The complete field guide
Essential
Engine off
Switch off 30m before composing. Vibration destroys sharpness at 400mm+.
Beanbag
Window beanbag outperforms any tripod in a vehicle. Non-negotiable for 500mm.
Position
Sun behind you or 45-degrees to side. Never shoot into direct sun unless silhouette is the goal.
Patience
A stationary vehicle with engine off is invisible to wildlife. Stay for 20 minutes minimum at any quality sighting - the behaviour develops.
Species
